identitySentence: Sarah Roland, Attorney at Law is a Denton criminal defense firm where founding attorney Sarah Roland has defended people facing charges across Denton County since she began practicing law in 2006.

concept.headline: Facing criminal charges in Denton County? Start your defense right now.
concept.subheadline: An arrest or charge is frightening, and the first hours matter. Tell us the basics of your situation and our office will follow up with real consultation options and a clear next step.
credibilityPoints: Founding attorney Sarah E. Roland has practiced law in Texas since 2006
A 100% criminal defense practice, at trial and on appeal
Selected as a Texas Super Lawyer every year from 2014 through 2026
Won a 2017 appellate reversal and a 2019 not-guilty verdict at retrial in a Denton County prosecution

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: The firm defends people charged with DWI and DUI, drug offenses, assault, sexual offenses, weapons charges, theft, and homicide. It also handles juvenile defense, probation revocations and motions to adjudicate, expunctions and nondisclosures, early release, appeals, and tickets.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: The firm practices from its office at 903 N Elm Street in Denton, serving clients across Denton, Collin, and Cooke counties. Criminal defense is a family tradition: Sarah Roland's late father, George Roland, was also a Denton criminal defense lawyer.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: When you reach out, the firm will ask about the charge, the county, any upcoming court dates, and whether anyone is currently in custody. Your first conversation is a free consultation, and you will get an honest assessment of your situation and what representation could look like.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

Dear Sarah Roland, Attorney at Law,

Your firm's record includes a rare result: a 2017 appellate reversal in the Fort Worth Court of Appeals, followed by a not-guilty verdict at retrial in January 2019 in a Denton County prosecution.

We also noticed a deliberate choice on your website: you publish no contact form, because a phone call gets faster answers in a criminal matter. That judgment is right for the person ready to call. But someone facing a DWI, a drug charge, or a juvenile case at 11 p.m. often will not dial a number. They will type a few sentences into a form if one exists, and if none exists, they move on to the next firm's site.

That specific gap is what our Lead Capturer closes. It is a custom contact form built for your firm alone, introduced by a custom identity sentence written from your verified record: "Sarah Roland, Attorney at Law is a Denton criminal defense firm where founding attorney Sarah Roland has defended people facing charges across Denton County since she began practicing law in 2006."

Here is how it works:

- Every inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox. Nothing sits in a dashboard your staff has to remember to check.
- The potential client automatically receives a response specific to their message, so a DWI inquiry gets a DWI-appropriate reply rather than a generic acknowledgment.
- The response presents your available consultation times.
- A $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, holding the slot for someone committed to showing up.

It does not replace your phone-first policy. It catches the people your phone line never hears from, and it runs on fixed rules rather than guesswork.

We would rather you judge evidence than our description, so we offer a 15-day proof period in which you can test the complete experience end to end. We make no revenue promises; we ask only that you watch what your own inbox does for 15 days.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information.
3. Confirm your firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject identifies your firm and the likely matter type.
6. Confirm Reply-To contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to the TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm the response is sent to the customer email without any staff action.
9. Confirm the customer email actually receives it.
10. Confirm it addresses the actual inquiry topic.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails — contact us and we will fix it before you owe anything.

The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
